reasoning: All three solvers give the correct final answer and essentially the same valid bijective argument: a strictly increasing 5-digit number is determined uniquely by choosing its 5 digits, and 0 cannot be included because it would have to be the leading digit, so the count is C(9,5)=126. The peer reviews were accurate, and none of the refinements needed substantive changes. Ranking: solver_1, solver_2, and solver_3 are effectively tied on logical correctness and justification quality; solver_1 is selected by a very narrow margin due to slightly more explicit arithmetic and slightly better-calibrated confidence, not because of any meaningful mathematical advantage.
